title: NOC 72429 – Other small engine and small equipment repairers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Small Engine and Equipment Repairers (NOC 72429) are the essential technical specialists maintaining the tools that power Canada’s landscaping, forestry, and urban maintenance sectors. As the country enforces stricter emissions standards and transitions toward professional-grade battery-electric tools, these repairers have moved beyond simple carburetor cleaning to advanced electronic control module (ECM) diagnostics and high-capacity battery cell testing. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they service everything from chainsaws and lawn tractors to specialized snow-removal equipment and industrial generators.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or 2+ years of experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Digital & Manual Diagnostics: Using 2026-grade handheld diagnostic scanners and traditional compression testers to isolate faults in fuel, ignition, and electrical systems.
Precision Repair: Repairing and overhauling small engines (2-cycle and 4-cycle), transmissions, and hydraulic systems to exact manufacturer tolerances.
Electrical Systems: Testing and repairing high-capacity battery packs, charging circuits, and electronic motor controllers on the latest generation of electric equipment.
Performance Calibration: Adjusting and "mapping" ignition and fuel systems for optimal performance and 2026-grade emission compliance.
Customer Advising: Discussing repair costs with clients, demonstrating proper maintenance of smart-features, and providing winterization/seasonal storage tips.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Vocational Training: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in Small Engine Repair or Industrial Mechanics is highly recommended.
Experience: Several years of on-the-job training or a combination of college and 2+ years of experience is usually required for senior roles.
Certification: Trade certification is voluntary in most provinces but is highly recommended for higher wages. In Ontario, certification is managed through Skilled Trades Ontario.

Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is available for "Small Engine Mechanics" in some provinces, allowing for national mobility and access to high-paying manufacturer-authorized service roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Good, with a steady need for technicians who can bridge the gap between traditional combustion engines and new electric platforms:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72429 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and In-Demand Skills streams. Minimum scores for regional draws (Southwestern and Eastern Ontario) started at 53, reflecting a critical need for mobile service technicians in agricultural and suburban hubs.

British Columbia: Strong demand. B.C. projects a high median wage of $32.00/hr, particularly in the "Mainland/Southwest" and "Vancouver Island" regions. The province is actively recruiting for technicians skilled in high-voltage battery diagnostics for commercial landscaping fleets.

Alberta: Competitive Earnings. Alberta reports a "High Hiring Difficulty" for specialized small engine mechanics. Top-tier earners in industrial and rental equipment maintenance in Calgary and Edmonton reach $45.00/hr.

National Trend: Over 91% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. Approximately 21% are self-employed, offering a clear pathway for newcomers to establish their own niche repair businesses or mobile service units.
Why Work in Canada as a Small Equipment Repairer?

Lead the Electric Tool Revolution: Gain hands-on experience with 2026-grade technologies, including brushless motor controllers, smart-charging infrastructure, and Bluetooth-enabled tool tracking systems.
Unbeatable Sector Diversity: Your skills are vital across multiple industries: Retail Trade (36%), specialized Repair & Maintenance (28%), and Landscaping Services (12%).
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your median annual salary is approximately $64,480, with specialized technicians in major hubs earning over $92,000.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is a frequent feature in OINP regional development draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72429 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern/Eastern Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience (up from 6 months in 2025) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for technicians with a job offer from a BC-based equipment dealership or landscaping firm, particularly in the "Fraser Valley" and "Okanagan" regions.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Small equipment technicians are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure and Service Workers."
